searchLedger.hypothesis: temp database/socket sandbox avoids live shard paths

searchActions.result: beforeEach creates a per-test os.tmpdir root at line 126, sets MEMORY_DB_PATH and MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS to that root at lines 128-129, and redirects SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR into the same temp tree at line 131. Each shard path is asserted to start with tempDir and not contain /mcp_server/database/ before destructive/copy operations at lines 171-175, 229-234, and 274-276, and afterEach closes handles and removes tempDir at lines 137-154.

searchLedger.rationale: No reviewed test path reads, writes, renames, or deletes under mcp_server/database/** or a production checkpoint directory.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: All destructive shard operations are derived from activeProfile.getVectorShardPath(tempDir) or dbPath under tempDir, and host socket path is sandboxed before handler use.

searchLedger.hypothesis: restart case is not merely a second attach on the same handle

searchActions.result: The test detaches, writes/moves the sentinel fixture, calls closeDb, resets degraded observability at lines 234-238, then opens unmanagedDb = new Database(dbPath) and attaches with that new handle at lines 248-250.

searchLedger.rationale: This rules out the narrow concern that the test only calls attach once on an already-open store; the sentinel read path is exercised after closing the singleton DB handle.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: It is still in-process rather than a module/process reload, but the test does not rely on an in-memory repair flag because it fabricates only the on-disk sentinel and resets observability before the new attach.

searchLedger.hypothesis: no banned ephemeral labels found in introduced code comments

searchActions.result: The added sentinel/restart/reindex comments do not include spec paths, packet/phase numbers, ADR/REQ/TASK ids, or similar labels. Existing comments such as HIGH-004/C1/BUG-012 appear outside the reviewed diff and were not introduced by commit 1ee9e1e767.

searchLedger.rationale: No hygiene hard-block found in the changed code/comments under review.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Commit diff for the reviewed files shows no newly added banned labels in code comments.

title: clear-degraded coverage does not protect pending repairs from normal reindex completion
evidence: The test seeds only in-memory degraded observability via recordVectorShardProbeFailure/recordVectorShardQuarantined/recordVectorShardRebuildStarted at lines 277-283, then starts a normal startReindexForTest at line 290 and expects healthy at lines 293-299. It never creates a repair-pending sentinel or live repair job. In the code under test, non-repair completion for a matching shard calls completeVectorShardRecovery at lib/embedders/reindex.ts:658-660, which records completion and unconditionally clears the repair sentinel at lib/embedders/reindex.ts:145-148. recordVectorShardRebuildCompleted also clears activeJobId when lastShard matches, even if the job id differs, at lib/observability/retrieval-observability.ts:245-252.
rationale: A normal reindex can therefore clear pending repair intent/degraded state for the same shard while another repair is still pending; this test would stay green because it models only a stale/lost repair id, not the pending-repair invariant requested by the phase lens.

title: sentinel-before-quarantine ordering is not crash-tested
evidence: The corrupted-shard test calls attachActiveVectorShard at line 190, waits until the async repair has started at line 191, and only then asserts the sentinel exists at line 197. The production order is write sentinel then rename shard at lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:590-591, but the test has no failpoint around fs.renameSync/quarantine and no restart between rename and sentinel write.
rationale: If the implementation regressed to rename first and write the sentinel afterwards, this test could still pass in a non-crashing run. It does not cover the crash-between-rename-and-sentinel durability gap called out in the review lens.
